z1Test that sys.modules is used properly by import.
util
MethodTypec
UseCachea
When it comes to sys.modules, import prefers it over anything else.
    Once a name has been resolved, sys.modules is checked to see if it contains
    the module desired. If so, then it is returned [use cache]. If it is not
    found, then the proper steps are taken to perform the import, but
    sys.modules is still used to return the imported module (e.g., not what a
    loader returns) [from cache on return]. This also applies to imports of
    things contained within a package and thus get assigned as an attribute
    [from cache to attribute] or pulled in thanks to a fromlist import
    [from cache for fromlist]. But if sys.modules contains None then
    ImportError is raised [None in cache].
    c
some module found!Z
some_module)
uncache
modules
__import__
assertEqual
selfZ
module_to_use
module
@/usr/lib64/python3.9/test/test_importlib/import_/test_caching.py
test_using_cache
UseCache.test_using_cachec
using_None)
assertRaises
ImportErrorr	
Z	exception
name)
test_None_in_cache 
UseCache.test_None_in_cacheN)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__
__doc__r
ImportlibUseCacheZ
SourceN)
return_c
fullname
original_loadr
load_module8
z2ImportlibUseCache.create_mock.<locals>.load_module)
mock_modulesr
names
mockr
create_mock5
ImportlibUseCache.create_mockc
	meta_path)
import_stater	
test_using_cache_after_loader@
